user_query:
I'm preparing an online module that ties early radar engineering to WWII history. I want to include a small, reproducible analysis of how carrier frequency affects detection range using the radar range equation, and then fold the results into the lesson draft.

Please do the following using the files in this workspace:

- Inspect scripts/simulate_radar.py and config/radar_config.json to understand how the configuration is used by the script. Do not change the script logic. In your status report (see deliverables), include a short mapping of at least three config fields to the exact variable names used in the script (e.g., tx_power_W -> tx_power_W, antenna_gain_linear -> …, snr_threshold_dB -> …) so it’s clear you verified how parameters flow into the calculation.
- Run the simulation so that it writes outputs/radar_results.csv using the provided configuration. If the outputs/ directory doesn’t exist, ensure it is created and the CSV is generated. The CSV should include at least: frequency_MHz, wavelength_m, tx_power_W, antenna_gain_linear, radar_cross_section_m2, noise_temperature_K, bandwidth_Hz, noise_figure_linear, system_losses_linear, snr_threshold_dB, max_range_km.
- Edit docs/module_lesson.md by replacing only the placeholder content between the markers <!-- INJECT_RESULTS_START --> and <!-- INJECT_RESULTS_END -->. Keep the markers themselves. Replace the TODO block with a brief results summary that:
  • States which frequency achieved the largest max_range_km and the value rounded to two decimals.
  • Provides a ranked list of the configured frequencies from longest to shortest range based on outputs/radar_results.csv.
  • Includes a relative link to outputs/radar_results.csv.
  Use the values computed in outputs/radar_results.csv; do not handwave or estimate.
- Compose a concise reports/status_update.md that includes:
  • A “Config-to-Code Mapping” section listing at least three mappings from config/radar_config.json fields to the exact variable names used in scripts/simulate_radar.py.
  • A “Data Summary” section giving the CSV header fields as written, the number of data rows, the ordered list of frequencies from longest to shortest range, and the single frequency/value pair that has the largest max_range_km (rounded to two decimals).
  • A “Lesson Update” section confirming that only the content between INJECT_RESULTS_START/INJECT_RESULTS_END in docs/module_lesson.md was changed and that it reflects the CSV results.

Deliverables and paths:
- Generated CSV at: outputs/radar_results.csv
- Updated lesson at: docs/module_lesson.md (only the marked block content updated; markers retained)
- Status report at: reports/status_update.md

All work should be reproducible locally without external dependencies beyond the standard Python runtime. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
